No Poverty.reason: The course content and objectives do not directly or indirectly address poverty eradication, social protection, or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: The course does not relate to food security, nutrition, agricultural productivity, or sustainable food systems.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The syllabus does not mention any applications of probability models or statistical methods to health-related issues or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate-level university course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with high-level mathematical and data analysis skills for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: 具備高階數理能力 (To equip with of high-level mathematical abilities)
熟悉統計計算與資料分析之能力 (to equip with statistical computing and data analysis abilities)

Gender Equality.reason: The course content, objectives, and core competencies do not address gender equality, discrimination, violence, or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: There is no mention of water resources, sanitation, pollution reduction, or water management in the course description or objectives.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: The course does not cover energy access, renewable energy, energy efficiency, or related technologies.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course equips students with advanced statistical methods, problem-solving, and innovation skills, directly supporting technological upgrading and higher economic productivity (Target 8.2).
evidence: 創新統計方法 (To innovate statistical methods)
解決統計相關問題 (to solve problems related to statistics)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly aims to develop abilities to "innovate statistical methods" and "develop statistical theories," directly enhancing scientific research and innovation (Target 9.5).
evidence: 創新統計方法、發展統計理論 (To innovate statistical methods and methodology, and to develop statistical theories)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course content does not address income inequality, social inclusion, discriminatory practices, or migration policies.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The syllabus does not contain any elements related to urban planning, housing, transport, heritage, or disaster resilience in communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course does not address sustainable consumption, resource efficiency, waste management, or corporate sustainability practices.

Climate Action.reason: The course covers stochastic processes and simulation techniques, which are foundational tools for modeling climate-related hazards and strengthening resilience (Target 13.1).

Life Below Water.reason: The course content does not relate to marine ecosystems, ocean pollution, sustainable fisheries, or ocean acidification.

Life on Land.reason: There is no mention of terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, deforestation, desertification, or land degradation in the syllabus.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly aims to equip students with "knowledge of professional ethics," which is a foundational element for developing accountable institutions (Target 16.6).
evidence: 具備專業倫理之知能 (To equip with knowledge of professional ethics)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course develops strong statistical computing and data analysis abilities, crucial for capacity building in data collection and monitoring progress on SDGs (Targets 17.18, 17.19).
evidence: 熟悉統計計算與資料分析之能力 (to equip with statistical computing and data analysis abilities)
解決統計相關問題 (to solve problems related to statistics)
